title: Pipeline Parallelism
description: Split transformer layers across GPUs like an assembly line — from naive schedules to 1F1B, minimizing the pipeline bubble.
tags: Pipeline Parallelism Basics: Splitting Layers Across GPUs
The 1F1B Schedule: One Forward One Backward
Pipeline Parallelism in Practice: Balancing Layers and Choosing Microbatches

notebooks.title: Pipeline Bubble
notebooks.objective: Simulate the naive pipeline schedule, measure bubble fractions analytically and via simulation, and visualize pipeline timelines.

notebooks.title: GPipe and 1F1B
notebooks.objective: Implement GPipe and 1F1B schedulers from scratch, compare their identical bubble fractions, and discover 1F1B's dramatic memory advantage.

notebooks.title: Interleaved Pipeline
notebooks.objective: Build the interleaved 1F1B schedule with virtual pipeline stages, verify the v-factor bubble reduction, and analyze communication trade-offs.

curator.name: Dr. Rajat Dandekar
curator.title: Course Instructor
curator.bio: Dr. Rajat Dandekar is a researcher and educator specializing in AI/ML, with a passion for making complex concepts accessible through intuitive explanations and hands-on learning.
